creator: ohartmann@walstatt.org
text:
On Thu, 06 Jun 2013 11:06:42 -0500
Stephen Montgomery-Smith <stephen@missouri.edu> wrote:

> Have you tried building it again?
> 
> One of the problems I found was that in the build of maxima, for some
> reason the build process started eating up resources until the
> computer was starved.  This could create random errors (e.g. a
> parallel build might have one of its compiles break), and sometimes
> this error would manifest itself in the most bizarre manner.  I have
> since applied a band-aid fix to this problem.
> 
> If you could try building it again, and report back, I would really
> appreciate it.


I tried this morning. Portstree is at revision Rev. 320433. This time,
on all boxes running FreeBSD 10.0-CURRENT I tried on, I receive the
error message bewlow, This time the failure is in another package.

MAXIMA build fine for me, without any complications. it seems more to
be an erratic failure. I'll try within the week on a FreeBSD 9.1-STABLE
box.
